source: Only 17.4 per cent of the households surveyed in 1971 had piped water,¹ and the figure for the three northern districts was lower still, at 6 per cent. The engineer who compiled the returns noted in his covering letter that he had counted a standpipe shared by nine families as a single connection, which is exactly the sort of decision that passes into every later table while the letter explaining it stays in the box it was filed in.

expect.why: A superscript footnote marker would be narrated as a stray number.

traps.span: a standpipe shared by nine families as a single connection
traps.label: counting-rule-must-stay

prepared: Only 17.4 per cent of the households surveyed in 1971 had piped water, and the figure for the three northern districts was lower still, at 6 per cent. The engineer who compiled the returns noted in his covering letter that he had counted a standpipe shared by nine families as a single connection, which is exactly the sort of decision that passes into every later table while the letter explaining it stays in the box it was filed in.
notes: The footnote marker rides directly on a decimal statistic. A model that cleans up the number along with the marker rounds, drops, or reorders a figure the whole chapter turns on.
